macro_events.event_summary: European markets set to open lower as prospects for a U.S.-Iran peace/nuclear deal fade, raising Middle East risk premium and supporting oil prices.
primary_drivers: Higher geopolitical risk premium in crude oil
Reduced probability of Iranian barrels returning to global supply
Defense spending tailwind from prolonged Middle East tension

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major with ~60% upstream earnings leverage; every $5/bbl sustained move in Brent flows directly to upstream FCF. Failed Iran deal removes the supply-overhang scenario.
Same upstream crude leverage as XOM; direct beneficiary of higher Brent on persistent Iran sanctions risk and removed supply path.
Pure-play US shale with highest crude price torque among large caps; Permian production unhedged in 2026 means near-1:1 EBITDA sensitivity to WTI.
Lockheed makes THAAD, PAC-3 Patriot interceptors and F-35s — the exact systems being consumed/deployed in Israel-Iran confrontation scenarios. Failed diplomacy extends restock orders.
Raytheon produces Patriot/SM-3/SM-6 interceptors used against Iranian missile/drone threats; ongoing tension sustains FMS backlog.
Oilfield services lever — higher sustained crude price pulls forward US shale completions activity where Halliburton is the #1 pressure pumper.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~20-25% of airline opex; Delta historically least hedged among US legacies, so a sustained crude bid directly compresses unit margins.
United has largest international/long-haul exposure among US carriers — fuel burn per ASM highest, and Middle East airspace risk also impacts routing costs.
Southwest unhedged on fuel since 2022 program wind-down; pure passthrough of higher crude into Q2/Q3 cost guide.
Cruise operator fuel costs rise with bunker prices; Middle East itinerary cancellations (Red Sea/Suez) already a headwind, reinforced by failed de-escalation.
Air freight fuel surcharges lag spot moves; near-term margin pressure plus weaker risk sentiment hits cyclical transport demand reads.

headlines: European stocks to open lower as hopes for U.S.-Iran peace deal fade
